# v2.4.0

This release keeps the evaluation weights unchanged and improves the Rust shogi engine around search, USI behavior, and benchmark analysis.

## Weight

- Uses the same weight as `v2.1.0`: `policy_weights.binary`
- SHA256: `8d2ad6ebd65afd9bdd921f7c03205582421f00cbe2c83ccbda984fbbe13747b3`
- No new training weight was adopted in this release.

## Strength Check

`v2.4.0` was validated against the `v2.1.0` baseline with the same weight file.

```text
seed 2401 / 100 games: 77-20-3
total score rate: 78.50%
95% CI: 70.63%..86.37%

seed 2801 / 100 games: 83-16-1
total score rate: 83.50%
95% CI: 76.29%..90.71%
```

## Changes

- Handle checked repetition as perpetual check loss.
- Respect USI `stop` during search.
- Improve USI `go` time-limit handling.
- Record benchmark games and end reasons.
- Summarize end reasons and paired starts in `record_analyze`.
- Show tail evaluation swings and largest tail drops in record analysis.
- Prioritize checking moves in search ordering.
- Use SEE to order tactical captures.
- Use `Position::is_check_move` in search ordering and qsearch filtering to reduce check-detection overhead.

## Notes

The root one-ply mate experiment was tested but not included. It lost to the previous `v2.4.0` candidate in direct comparison, so the release keeps the safer search behavior.

Remaining work:

- Classify `BaselineWin` records with large late evaluation drops.
- Reduce remaining `PerpetualCheckLoss` cases.
- Investigate side-to-move bias in the 100-game benchmarks.
